spec_match.rationale: Tracer surfaces the partition events and timeline, but the cross-partition step is simulated (no real different-top-level-site iframe available) so what the user sees on the timeline is a synthetic event, not a real spec'd failure.

interactive.rationale: Create / fetch / revoke buttons drive a timeline with timestamps — much more than a code card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index blurb promises a traced timeline of partition events; the page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers create / cross-fetch / revoke but no race conditions, no concurrent partition probe, no inheritance via Worker.

references.rationale: The page now includes a see-also section with the ChromeStatus feature 5130361898795008, the Storage Partitioning explainer, and sibling concepts.

openQuestions.title: Label the synthetic cross-partition event
openQuestions.detail: The timeline today shows a `cross-partition fetch blocked` event that is in reality just a synthetic failure injected by the page. Either label it `(simulated)` or wire it to a sandboxed iframe so the failure is real.

openQuestions.title: Add a Worker / SharedWorker lane
openQuestions.detail: Blob URL access from a Worker inherits the partition; Workers are a common source of `why is my blob broken` questions. Show the lifecycle inside a Worker.

summary: Best UX in the feature with a timeline and lifecycle events, but it should label the synthetic cross-partition failure more explicitly and add worker coverage.
